text.content:
As forklift technology transforms modern material handling fleets, energy efficiency has become a competitive advantage. While battery technology and charging infrastructure receive significant attention, one critical factor often goes overlooked: your forklift tires.

Tires with lower rolling resistance outperform standard options across every metric that matters to your operation. They consume less energy. They last longer. They generate less heat. And they deliver measurable savings in downtime and operating costs.

This guide explains why low rolling resistance industrial tires are the superior choice for material handling applications and how MAXAM's advanced rubber compound technology sets the new standard for performance.

rich_text.plain_text:
As forklift technology transforms modern material handling fleets, energy efficiency has become a competitive advantage. While battery technology and charging infrastructure receive significant attention, one critical factor often goes overlooked: your forklift tires.

Tires with lower rolling resistance outperform standard options across every metric that matters to your operation. They consume less energy. They last longer. They generate less heat. And they deliver measurable savings in downtime and operating costs.

This guide explains why low rolling resistance industrial tires are the superior choice for material handling applications and how MAXAM's advanced rubber compound technology sets the new standard for performance.

text.content: What Makes Low Rolling Resistance Tires Better

rich_text.plain_text: What Makes Low Rolling Resistance Tires Better

text.content:
Rolling resistance is the energy a tire consumes through internal friction within its materials. When a forklift operator moves heavy loads across the warehouse floor, the motor works continuously to overcome this resistance.

Standard tires waste significant energy through:

rich_text.plain_text:
Rolling resistance is the energy a tire consumes through internal friction within its materials. When a forklift operator moves heavy loads across the warehouse floor, the motor works continuously to overcome this resistance.

Standard tires waste significant energy through:

text.content: Excessive friction in the rubber compound

rich_text.plain_text: Excessive friction in the rubber compound

text.content: Poor heat dissipation that increases internal resistance

rich_text.plain_text: Poor heat dissipation that increases internal resistance

text.content: Inefficient tread patterns that create unnecessary drag

rich_text.plain_text: Inefficient tread patterns that create unnecessary drag

text.content: Suboptimal construction that flexes and squirms under load

rich_text.plain_text: Suboptimal construction that flexes and squirms under load

text.content: Low rolling resistance tires minimize these energy losses through superior engineering. The result is a tire that works with your equipment, not against it.

rich_text.plain_text: Low rolling resistance tires minimize these energy losses through superior engineering. The result is a tire that works with your equipment, not against it.

text.content: 1. Better Energy Efficiency

rich_text.plain_text: 1. Better Energy Efficiency

text.content:
The most direct benefit of low rolling resistance is reduced energy consumption. When a tire requires less force to roll, your forklift's motor draws less power.

For electric fleets running order pickers and counterbalanced trucks, this means:

rich_text.plain_text:
The most direct benefit of low rolling resistance is reduced energy consumption. When a tire requires less force to roll, your forklift's motor draws less power.

For electric fleets running order pickers and counterbalanced trucks, this means:

text.content: Extended battery life between charges

rich_text.plain_text: Extended battery life between charges

text.content: Longer shift durations

rich_text.plain_text: Longer shift durations

text.content: Reduced charging frequency

rich_text.plain_text: Reduced charging frequency

text.content: Lower electricity costs

rich_text.plain_text: Lower electricity costs

text.content: For operations using internal combustion equipment, the savings appear as improved fuel economy and reduced emissions. Every gallon of propane or diesel saved through efficient tires directly improves your bottom line.

rich_text.plain_text: For operations using internal combustion equipment, the savings appear as improved fuel economy and reduced emissions. Every gallon of propane or diesel saved through efficient tires directly improves your bottom line.

text.content: 2. Longer Tire Life

rich_text.plain_text: 2. Longer Tire Life

text.content:
Low rolling resistance tires generate less internal friction, which directly reduces heat buildup. Heat is the primary enemy of rubber tires since it accelerates compound degradation, softens materials, and leads to premature failure.

By running cooler, low rolling resistance tires:

rich_text.plain_text:
Low rolling resistance tires generate less internal friction, which directly reduces heat buildup. Heat is the primary enemy of rubber tires since it accelerates compound degradation, softens materials, and leads to premature failure.

By running cooler, low rolling resistance tires:

text.content: Delay the onset of uneven wear

rich_text.plain_text: Delay the onset of uneven wear

text.content: 3. Reduced Downtime

rich_text.plain_text: 3. Reduced Downtime

text.content:
Downtime is the hidden cost that erodes profitability in material handling. When tires fail, equipment stops. Loads sit. Schedules slip.

Low rolling resistance tires reduce downtime through:

text.content: How to keep Low Rolling Resistance Tires Performing at Their best

rich_text.plain_text: How to keep Low Rolling Resistance Tires Performing at Their best

text.content: Even the best forklift tires require proper care to deliver full benefits. Implement these practices across your fleet.

text.content: Regular Tire Maintenance

rich_text.plain_text: Regular Tire Maintenance

text.content: Consistent tire inspection protocols catch problems before they escalate:

rich_text.plain_text: Consistent tire inspection protocols catch problems before they escalate:

text.content: Check for internal damage or separations

rich_text.plain_text: Check for internal damage or separations

text.content: Remove embedded debris from tread patterns

rich_text.plain_text: Remove embedded debris from tread patterns

text.content: Monitor for uneven wear indicating alignment issues

rich_text.plain_text: Monitor for uneven wear indicating alignment issues

text.content: Look for signs of heat buildup or compound degradation

rich_text.plain_text: Look for signs of heat buildup or compound degradation

text.content: Proper Load Management

rich_text.plain_text: Proper Load Management

text.content: Always respect load capacity ratings and ensure even load distribution. Excess pressure on individual tires:

rich_text.plain_text: Always respect load capacity ratings and ensure even load distribution. Excess pressure on individual tires:

text.content: Increases rolling resistance

rich_text.plain_text: Increases rolling resistance

text.content: Accelerates tread wear

rich_text.plain_text: Accelerates tread wear

text.content: Generates unnecessary heat

rich_text.plain_text: Generates unnecessary heat

text.content: Reduces overall tire life

rich_text.plain_text: Reduces overall tire life

text.content: Wheel Alignment Verification

rich_text.plain_text: Wheel Alignment Verification

text.content: Misaligned wheels force tires to scrub against the direction of travel, dramatically increasing rolling resistance. Regular wheel alignment checks should be standard procedure.

rich_text.plain_text: Misaligned wheels force tires to scrub against the direction of travel, dramatically increasing rolling resistance. Regular wheel alignment checks should be standard procedure.

text.content: Correct Tire Selection

rich_text.plain_text: Correct Tire Selection

text.content:
Match your tire type to your specific application. Modern solid rubber tires dissipate heat better than polyurethane tires at higher speeds. Non-marking tires serve clean environments without sacrificing efficiency. Traction tires provide grip for operations involving ramps without excessive energy loss.

Here’s a simple, general guide that help get you started on selecting the type of forklift based on applications.

rich_text.plain_text:
Match your tire type to your specific application. Modern solid rubber tires dissipate heat better than polyurethane tires at higher speeds. Non-marking tires serve clean environments without sacrificing efficiency. Traction tires provide grip for operations involving ramps without excessive energy loss.

Here’s a simple, general guide that help get you started on selecting the type of forklift based on applications.

rich_text.plain_text: Examples of Low Rolling Resistance Tires

text.content: Your material handling equipment deserves the very best when it comes to efficiency, performance, and reliability. Below, we look at some examples of low rolling resistance forklift tire:

text.content: MS600

rich_text.plain_text: MS600

text.content: The MS600 is engineered to extract peak performance from forklifts. At its core is EcoPoint3, MAXAM’s advanced compounding technology that dramatically reduces rolling resistance, meaning

rich_text.plain_text: The MS600 is engineered to extract peak performance from forklifts. At its core is EcoPoint3, MAXAM’s advanced compounding technology that dramatically reduces rolling resistance, meaning

text.content:
your forklift consumes less energy or fuel per shift and runs cooler under continuous operation.

Less friction means less heat, and less heat means longer tire life and fewer battery swaps. The result is a solid resilient tire that maximizes uptime, lowers operating costs, and keeps your material handling fleet moving efficiently, hour after hour.

your forklift consumes less energy or fuel per shift and runs cooler under continuous operation.

text.content: MS700

rich_text.plain_text: MS700

text.content:
The MS700 takes the efficiency of EcoPoint3 and pairs it with a stable, three‑stage construction designed for heavy loads and demanding industrial applications. Low rolling resistance reduces energy waste, helping electric and internal combustion forklifts alike stretch their shifts further, while the advanced compound dissipates heat to protect against premature wear.

With a puncture‑proof solid design and tread that maintains grip on smooth warehouse floors or rougher surfaces, the MS700 delivers the durability and performance that high‑cycle operations demand, all backed by the science of EcoPoint3.

Whether your operation requires press-on tires for indoor warehouses, solid pneumatic for recycling centers, or specialized non-marking tires for clean environments, MAXAM delivers low rolling resistance solutions engineered for superior results.

Ready to experience the difference? Contact your local MAXAM representative to discuss your specific material handling application and discover why low rolling resistance forklift tires are the better choice for your fleet.
